PR: PR-WB-01 docs(workbench): architecture and source-of-truth policy
Acceptance Criteria: Architecture, scope, and view-model docs exist under docs/workbench and explicitly state that Workbench is an orchestration/UI layer that does not own compiler, verifier, VM, runtime, or boundary semantics.

PR: PR-WB-02 feat(workbench): bootstrap app shell
Acceptance Criteria: apps/workbench starts as a Tauri desktop shell with React and TypeScript, basic routes and layout exist, and no Semantic logic is duplicated in the UI layer.

PR: PR-WB-03 feat(workbench): command bus and cli adapter
Acceptance Criteria: A deterministic job model and command bus exist, CLI execution goes only through a process adapter for smc, svm, cargo, and release scripts, and Workbench does not call private crate internals.

PR: PR-WB-04 feat(workbench): project open and workspace settings
Acceptance Criteria: Users can open a workspace, persist recent projects and settings, and the selected project becomes the root context for all Workbench jobs without introducing alternate project metadata semantics.

PR: PR-WB-05 feat(workbench): operations cockpit
Acceptance Criteria: Overview shows branch, commit, baseline tag, test/build/bundle states, readiness summary, and known limits derived from real commands and repository documents rather than hardcoded UI state.

PR: PR-WB-06 feat(workbench): jobs panel and command output
Acceptance Criteria: Users can run smc compile, smc run, svm run, svm disasm, cargo test --workspace, and verify_release_bundle.ps1 from the UI and inspect structured job history and output.

PR: PR-WB-07 feat(workbench): spec navigator
Acceptance Criteria: Workbench displays docs/spec and docs/roadmap as a navigable tree with file-level viewing and search entrypoints while treating repository docs as the only source of truth.

PR: PR-WB-08 feat(workbench): release map and readiness panel
Acceptance Criteria: Readiness, compatibility, release checklist, smoke matrix, and known-limit panels are visible in UI with source paths and freshness indicators, without any alternative release scoring model.

PR: PR-WB-09 feat(workbench): project explorer and editor shell
Acceptance Criteria: File tree, multi-tab editor, open/save/reload, and dirty markers work for .sm files, and the editor remains a shell rather than a full IDE or second parser.

PR: PR-WB-10 feat(workbench): compile and check current file actions
Acceptance Criteria: Users can compile and check the current file from the editor shell, and outputs route into the shared jobs/diagnostics pipeline without bypassing the CLI adapter.

PR: PR-WB-11 feat(workbench): diagnostics model and panel
Acceptance Criteria: Diagnostics are grouped by parse, type, module, verify, and runtime families with preserved code, severity, range, and file fields and can be opened from command results.

PR: PR-WB-12 feat(workbench): diagnostics spec links
Acceptance Criteria: Diagnostics panel supports search by error code and links to related spec sections and error-family docs, including import/export module codes where available.

PR: PR-WB-13 feat(workbench): formatter integration via smc fmt
Acceptance Criteria: Format File, Format Project, and Format Check call only the canonical smc fmt surface, support a preview or diff flow, and clearly mark the formatter as draft or stable according to repo policy.

PR: PR-WB-14 feat(workbench): disasm and verify inspector
Acceptance Criteria: Workbench renders disassembly and verifier results in dedicated panels using real svm disasm and verified-path outputs without inventing a second bytecode or verifier model.

PR: PR-WB-15 feat(workbench): trace and runtime inspection
Acceptance Criteria: Trace, quota, capability, and runtime summaries are visible as inspection views driven by existing Semantic outputs and do not reimplement VM or runtime semantics in the UI.

PR: PR-WB-16 feat(workbench): release console
Acceptance Criteria: One screen shows release gates, artifact list, known limits, docs alignment, and asset-smoke status, and every status is derived from real checks or repository docs rather than manual toggles.

PR: PR-WB-17 feat(workbench): one-click validation run
Acceptance Criteria: Workbench can trigger the clean validation pass and export a release report using existing commands and scripts, and release-valid is computed only from actual gate results.

PR: PR-WB-18 feat(workbench): project scaffolding
Acceptance Criteria: Workbench can create or initialize a project with the canonical package layout, manifest, starter sources, and starter smoke file without introducing nonstandard project structure.

PR: PR-WB-19 feat(workbench): docs entry and package metadata preview
Acceptance Criteria: Users can open a docs landing page and inspect package metadata and manifest previews as read-only presentation derived from repository files and package documents.

PR: PR-WB-20 feat(workbench): smlsp bridge
Acceptance Criteria: Workbench integrates a canonical editor-facing protocol through smlsp for hover, go-to-definition, inline diagnostics, and formatting, and does not invent a parallel editor semantics layer.
